prompt:
Platform-inventory ticket, Ruby. Compliance wants a monthly inventory of every project on our self-managed GitLab, and the old rake task that hammered page=1..N fell over once we passed a few tens of thousands of projects — GitLab's own docs say big listings should use a different pagination style, so let's do it properly this time. Build the tool as plain Ruby (stdlib only, zero gems): gitlab_client.rb plus project_inventory.rb (split further if you like). ruby test_keyset_projects.rb is the acceptance contract; it runs a loopback fake GitLab, so no real instance and no real tokens anywhere.

Research first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official GitLab docs — at minimum the REST API overview (auth header, BOTH pagination styles and their exact parameters/response headers, what marks the end of a keyset collection, the moved-endpoint/Location behavior, and the note about null booleans) and the Projects API page (which order_by works for keyset listing, the id_after attribute, and the threshold where GitLab tells you offset paging stops being viable). First-party docs.gitlab.com pages only — no blogs, no Stack Overflow as sources. Our pinned wire contract is docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json; both are protected along with the test file. If the live docs contradict anything pinned, flag it in your summary and leave the protected files alone. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

What the tests drive:
- GitlabClient.new(base_url:, token:) — documented token header on every request. Errors are typed: AuthError for 401, ApiError (with .status) otherwise, RedirectLoopError when redirect-following exceeds a sane bound; no error message may ever contain the token. When GitLab says a resource moved, do what the docs say: hit the endpoint in the Location header (still authenticated), bounded hops.
- ProjectInventory.new(client) with fetch_all(per_page:, mode: :keyset default or :offset) — keyset mode sends exactly the documented controls (pagination/order_by/sort/per_page, ordered by id ascending) and then follows the next-page URL the server hands back IN THE HEADER, byte-for-byte — our fake deliberately shuffles the parameter order in those URLs, so rebuilding the query yourself will fail. Stop when the server signals the end the documented way. Offset mode walks the numeric-page headers until the next-page header comes back empty. Both return the raw project hashes in order.
- fetch_project(namespaced_path) — single project lookup with the namespaced path encoded the way the docs require, following a moved-resource response to its new endpoint.
- report(projects) — {"rows" => [...], "summary" => {...}}: rows sorted by id carrying id, path_with_namespace, visibility, archived, emails_disabled. Careful with emails_disabled: GitLab's docs are explicit that some boolean fields can be null and null is NOT false — a null must survive into the row (JSON null, key present) and the summary counts {"true"/"false"/"unknown"} in emails_disabled_counts plus "total".

test_keyset_projects.rb and everything under docs/ are protected; don't touch them.

research.interface: GitLab REST API v4 projects endpoint with keyset pagination

official_sources.title: REST API | GitLab Docs
official_sources.used_for: base path /api/v4; PRIVATE-TOKEN header auth; keyset pagination via pagination=keyset with order_by/sort/per_page, next page delivered in the Link header (rel="next", carrying id_after=... for id ordering) with the end of collection marked by an absent Link header and empty array; offset pagination headers x-next-page, x-page, x-per-page, x-total, x-total-pages, x-prev-page; moved endpoints respond with a message and the new endpoint in the Location header ('moved permanently', GA in GitLab 16.7); null boolean fields are neither true nor false; NAMESPACE/PROJECT_PATH must be URL-encoded (%2F)

official_sources.title: Projects API | GitLab Docs
official_sources.used_for: GET /projects attributes including order_by (id supported for keyset), sort asc/desc, id_after filter, membership/owned/simple; guidance to use keyset-based pagination to list more than 50,000 projects; project response fields id, path_with_namespace, visibility (private|internal|public), archived, and nullable booleans such as emails_disabled

research.language_rationale: The asset-inventory tooling this feeds is Ruby, and GitLab documents project listing as plain REST over /api/v4 with header auth, Link-header keyset pagination, and offset headers; Ruby's stdlib net/http and json exercise that documented surface hermetically with zero gems, so raw REST in Ruby is the credible integration path.
